The runtime builds a cache from causal byte patches and then emits two bytes per step.
The deployment artifact should contain only the patch model weights needed for inference.
A verifier must compare source and receiver logits before promoting transfer claims.
Median latency is more stable than a single timing sample on local desktop hardware.
The payload hash protects the portable domain from accidental mutation during migration.
The benchmark records artifact size, parameter memory, peak resident set size, and throughput.
Generation quality gates check printability, repetition, and held-out byte prediction.
Every promoted claim must name the comparator, dataset, device, and acceptance threshold.
